title: Standalone CatBoost CTR blend
summary: The current best relies on AutoGluon GBM/XGB; CatBoost is specifically designed for high-cardinality categoricals and ordered target statistics that reduce leakage.
rationale: The current best relies on AutoGluon GBM/XGB; CatBoost is specifically designed for high-cardinality categoricals and ordered target statistics that reduce leakage. Driver, Race, Compound, stint bins, and tyre-life bins are strong categorical interactions for pit behavior.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoostClassifier with raw categorical columns plus engineered categorical crosses such as Race_Stint, Driver_Compound, Race_Compound_TyreLifeBin, PositionBin_Compound. Use ordered boosting, moderate depth, class weights if needed, and blend rank-normalized predictions with the current GBM/XGB output.
expected_effect: Likely adds model-family diversity and better categorical priors, improving ensemble AUC more than another GBM feature tweak.
risk: CatBoost can overfit tiny race-driver combinations; group-aware validation is needed before trusting random CV gains.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
